summary:
The most common site of esophageal carcinoma varies globally but has shifted toward the **distal esophagus** and **gastroesophageal junction**, especially in Western populations due to rising rates of adenocarcinoma linked to chronic acid reflux and Barrett’s metaplasia. This shift makes the distal portion—particularly below the cardiac sphincter—the primary location for newly diagnosed cases today.

The summary above contains more than two sentences; please revise strictly within exactly two sentences as per requirement:

Final Summary: In Western countries, the most common site of esophageal cancer is the distal esophagus and gastroesophageal junction due to increased prevalence of adenocarcinoma from conditions like GERD and Barrett’s esophagus. Globally, while squamous cell carcinomas are still frequent in other regions, the rise of adenocarcinoma dominates modern epidemiology in high-income settings.
